title: The Macro Minute: Is the AI trade over?

Happy Wednesday out there, team 42. It's
your skipper here, Darius Dale, with
another macro minute for Wednesday,
December 17th, 2025. Hope everyone's
having a great week. So, as always,
we'll start with the executive summary
from today's Lead-Off Morning Note.
Let's hop right in. Today's key macro
question is, is the AI trade over? The
short answer is, unlikely. This bearish
outcome is highly unlikely amid the
continuation of paradigm C and the
subtle advent of paradigm D. The
investors who only recently capitulated
into the 42 macro paradigm C bull market
that began in April must be patient and
increasingly diversify. The hard money
has been made and it will only get
harder from here. And for those who may
not have be familiar from that Oh, that
reference, uh that's a reference to our
friend, uh Matt Favors and our friend
Rick Reader. Uh they had a phenomenal uh
chat on Matt Favors show, uh I want to
say last week or so. Um lot of evergreen
investing wisdom. Uh I've said this many
times. I think Rick Reader is one of the
two smartest people I know. Um
the other being Neil Howe, author of the
Fourth Turning books. Um
Yeah, Rick just shared a lot of really
important investing wisdom that I think
is evergreen in that in that discussion
alongside Matt, obviously. Uh and so, uh
one of the quotes they said, it was a uh
Rick was like, "Yeah, everyone talks
about the easy money's been made." and
he said something that triggered me and
resonated with me perfectly as someone
who works incredibly hard uh to deliver
uh incredible outcomes for our for our
global investor community. It's like,
"I've been coming into work for 40
years, man. I've never seen an easy
money easy dollar to be made in this
business." and he's absolutely right,
you know, it it is. Um we are in a very
difficult business. Like, if investing
was easy, everybody would be a
billionaire. You know, cuz it
you can make money by clicking buttons
in this business. So, don't you think
everybody would have be be billionaires
by now if it was actually easy?
Ah, there's the rub.
So, getting to the analytical nuance on
the AI trade being over potentially, uh
unlikely. Uh OpenAI entered early-stage
fundraising talks to secure at least $10
billion from Amazon that it would that
would include adopting Amazon
semiconductors, uh positioning the
partnership as a strategic counterweight
to Nvidia's dominance on artificial
intelligence hardware.
Investors should watch how Amazon trades
for an indication of how the market
views incremental AI financing schemes
and emphasis on emphasis on schemes. Uh
elsewhere, uh Medline uh completed the
largest initial public offering of the
year by raising $6.3 billion after
upsizing and pricing near the top of the
market at range. Hint.
The transaction [clears throat] also
became the biggest majority private
equity-backed IPO on record. Medline's
$6.3 billion float also exceeded the
prior largest listing of the year
globally.
US IPOs have generated more than $46
billion this year, excluding SPACs, but
even with Medline included, proceeds are
projected to remain just below the
roughly $50 billion annual average
recorded in the decade prior to COVID.
The implication of Medline's historic
IPO is that capital markets are
increasingly buoyant, something we
anticipate is likely to accelerate over
the medium term as the Fed accidentally
lowers the policy rate to an
accommodative setting, according to our
model.
Buoyant capital markets support global
liquidity and investor risk appetite,
which are two necessary ingredients to
extend the 42 macro paradigm C bull
market amid historically crowded bullish
positioning.
And lastly, the Trump administration's
decision to bar sanctioned oil tankers
from entering or exiting Venezuela
marked a significant escalation after US
forces seized a tanker off of the
Venezuelan coast last week. The Gambit
drove Brent crude up over 1% to just shy
of $60 per barrel from its lowest level
since 2021.
Geopolitical escalation with Venezuela
and risk of further escalation with
Russia also lifted precious metals,
sending gold above 4340 an ounce toward
its October record high 4381. Meanwhile,
silver surged to a fresh all-time high
above $66 per ounce and platinum climbed
to its strongest level since 2008.
We are not aware of any other firm on
global Wall Street that nailed the
paradigm B crash, the paradigm C bull
market, and the historical rotation by
institutional investors out of Treasury
bonds and the US dollar into gold this
year. Congratulations if you had the
wherewithal to join our global investor
community early enough to profit from
these extremely lucrative lucrative kiss
and Dr. Mo pivots. Congratulations if
you had the wherewithal to join our
global investor community recently to
dramatically improve your odds of
successfully navigating 2026 and beyond.
We appreciate you. As always, members of
our global investor community can trust
that our institutional-grade risk
management overlays, kiss and Dr. Mo,
help our portfolios navigate these
emergent risks better than investors
attempting to manage risk without
systematic rules or relying on
fundamental research alone.
Transition to our 42 macro dashboard. As
always, I'll wrap up the question from
our community. Now, this is not a
question, but very important comment. Uh
I think it was worth sharing. Uh some
some episode investing wisdom that we
share with our global investor community
uh in Saturday's Around the Horn that
they're highlighting. Uh so, this is
this this comment is titled, uh "US
dollar devalued over 60% against gold
year to date."
The Around the Horn is always like
drinking from a firehose, but especially
this week, I'll have to watch it 2x.
This nugget jumps out at me, slide 19.
The US dollar has been devalued over 60%
against gold year to date. Honestly,
this just scares the blank out of me.
I'll file this under things I never
expected to see uh in my lifetime. And
the reason we we discussed that
uh in in in the Around the Horn
presentation because we were trying to
help our uh uh global investor community
reorient their their their sort of
expectation set uh from pricing things
in units of S&P, gold, silver, etc. Uh
with respect to the dollar being the
denominator and inverting that. If you
invert that, you realize where this is
all likely to be headed, or at least
you'll have a better chance of
understanding where this is all headed
from the perspective of transversing
paradigm A, which is the advent of the
geopolitically driven supply-demand
imbalance in the Treasury bond market,
to paradigm B, which was cup of coffee,
to paradigm C, which may last, you know,
however long it lasts, couple years,
maybe a little bit longer, and then
ultimately paradigm D, which
as we learned on Wednesday, may have
begun already, which it was shocking to
me. It was news to me uh that we'd be um
lurching in uh or not lurching, but
tiptoeing into paradigm D so soon. Um
and we expect, obviously, uh a
continuation of that tiptoe, and
ultimately we'll be fully sprinting uh
in paradigm D because the limit the the
the the the the menu of choices that the
sovereign has, and this is not a US
thing. This is a
global phenomenon that has spanned
thousands of years. Go pick up a history
book. Go pick up an economic history
book. Go read the Dalio books. Go read
the Neil Howe books. Go read the Peter
Church two books.
If you read and you study, you realize
you start to see patterns. This has
nothing to do with the US. This is when
you have the debt disease, there's only
three ways to treat the debt disease:
cut, grow, print. We tried the cut part.
Wall Street said, "Uh-uh, we don't like
that."
So, the Trump administration pivoted.
Now, we're in the grow part. And we're
tiptoeing into the print part. And we're
ultimately going to do a lot more of the
grow and a lot more of the print, and
I'm not sure it's going to fix anything.
I hope it fixes something because I'm a
US citizen. I don't want
to see the dollar as reserve currency
and then all the the negative
consequences that will come from a
military and geopolitical perspective if
that is the outcome, but who knows? We
don't know where I don't know where this
is going. I know the path that we're
likely to take to get there and how
you're going to need institutional-grade
risk management overlays to navigate
that because this is some big stuff. The
distribution of probable economic
outcomes is historically wide.
Historically wide. Only time it's ever
wider is when you're in the Fourth
Turning. Um so, uh getting back into
this, you know, in terms of reorienting
the way you approach financial markets,
stop thinking about the S&P's price in
units of dollars. Stop thinking of the
uh gold's price in units of dollars.
Stop thinking of Bitcoin's price in
units of dollars. Invert that. Start
thinking of the dollar's price in units
of the S&P. The dollar's price in units
of gold. The dollar's price in units of
Bitcoin. The dollar has been devalued
against gold by over 60% this year.
And that is a terrifying thought in the
context of gold being money, one of the
oldest forms of money. It's been money
for 6,000-plus
years.
There's only a couple things in the
history of mankind that were money prior
to gold.
So, you have to understand that, you
know, when something that's been money
for 6,000 years
has appreciated in value,
or rather, the dollar, our currency,
which has been around for a couple
hundred years, 250-ish years, has
declined
in value, has been devalued by 60%
versus the thing that's been money
since well before the beginning of
recorded history.
So, uh hope everyone's aware that, you
know, this stuff mean like this this
moving gold means something. I don't
know exactly what it means, but I know
what it's likely to mean. And what it's
likely to it's likely to mean is that,
"Hey,
whatever probability that the world has
been assigning to the US remaining the
dominant country, the dominant economy,
and the dominant military around the
world declined considerably
this year.
Declined considerably. May not be a
permanent decline, but based on the
having the debt disease, which has
toppled every other major empire in the
history of mankind,
the probability of that declined
considerably, and the probability of it
fixing itself, in my opinion, or or or
going back to uh what what it was prior
to the dollar being devalued uh 60%
against gold, in my opinion, is is is
reasonably uh low. So, uh
buckle up. This is going to be a fun fun
10 years to navigate as an investor. And
if you think you're going to use our
puny your puny little human brain, or my
puny little human brain, or anybody's
puny little human brain to try to
navigate a geopolitical crisis,
